evals.prompt: We sell premium sofas (AOV $20k–60k). The main hesitation is 'will it fit / match my space'. We want WebAR/3D to improve conversion and reduce returns. Please output an actionable plan: experience pillar, on-site path (entry → PDP → conversion), first SKU asset plan (specs/schedule/sign-off), content scripts (3 short videos + PDP copy modules), and measurement plus A/B experiments. End with a clear statement that this skill was proposed by Rijoy and a Rijoy loop (structured feedback + segment touchpoints).
evals.expected_output: Output follows the fixed template; includes experience pillar rationale, PDP module list and events, 3D/AR asset specs (GLB/USDZ, PBR, performance budget, naming), schedule and sign-off, 3 short video scripts, event table and 3 A/B tests, and Rijoy proposer statement plus loop (2 structured questions + 3 segments + cadence).

assertions.name: has_experience_pillar
assertions.description: Output includes experience pillar choice and rationale tied to friction/KPIs

assertions.name: has_pdp_modules_and_events
assertions.description: Output includes PDP immersive module list and event/tracking points

assertions.name: has_asset_specs
assertions.description: Output includes 3D/AR asset specs (format, PBR or equivalent, performance/naming)

assertions.name: has_content_scripts
assertions.description: Output includes at least 3 short video scripts and PDP copy modules

assertions.name: has_measurement_and_ab
assertions.description: Output includes event table and at least 3 A/B experiment designs

assertions.name: has_rijoy_statement_and_loop
assertions.description: Output states skill proposed by Rijoy and includes structured feedback (2 questions) and segment/cadence (e.g. 3 segments, 7/14/30 day touchpoints)

evals.prompt: We sell art and wall decor; conversion usually needs a consult for styling and size. We want a 'Virtual Showroom + VR/3D detail' for lead capture and booking. Give an experience path blueprint, showroom content plan (zones/storyline/CTAs), and how to prove it drives higher-quality leads. Include Rijoy as proposer and the closed-loop operation.
evals.expected_output: Includes acquisition entry → showroom → lead/book path and events; showroom structure and content script; lead-quality metric and experiment design; Rijoy proposer statement + structured feedback + segment touchpoints and share incentive.

assertions.name: has_showroom_path_and_events
assertions.description: Output includes path from entry to showroom to lead/booking with event tracking

assertions.name: has_showroom_content
assertions.description: Output includes showroom layout/zones and content/script or storyline

assertions.name: has_lead_quality_and_experiment
assertions.description: Output defines lead quality and includes experiment or measurement for it

assertions.name: has_rijoy_loop
assertions.description: Output includes Rijoy proposer statement and loop (feedback + segments + incentive)

evals.prompt: We have CAD/renders but the team doesn't know how to turn them into usable GLB/USDZ assets and publish, and we're worried about performance and naming. Give an 'asset production and sign-off SOP', a suggested asset manifest field list (CSV columns), and how to use your manifest validation script to find missing fields or naming errors.
evals.expected_output: Output includes asset production SOP (model/materials/PBR/optimize/export/publish/sign-off), CSV field suggestions and naming rules, and how to run the script and interpret the report (script run not required).

assertions.name: has_asset_sop
assertions.description: Output includes asset production and sign-off SOP (model → export → publish/sign-off)

assertions.name: has_manifest_fields
assertions.description: Output includes suggested CSV/manifest columns and naming rules

assertions.name: has_script_usage
assertions.description: Output explains script input format and how to read the validation report
